conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Let me first review the official eligibility guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that. Now I have the information I need to help you.

To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 18 years old. I'm wondering if I can still get Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine if you can still get Child Benefit for Alex at 18 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

(For example, is he studying at school, college, or in an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in education at the moment. He finished school and hasn't gone on to college or anything like that. He's just at home with me right now figuring out his next steps.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have one more important question:

**Is Alex currently working, or has he started claiming any benefits in his own right** (such as Universal Credit, Employment and Support Allowance, or Jobseeker's Allowance)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at the moment, and he's not claiming any benefits either. He's just taking some time to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me also ask:

**Do you live in the UK with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. We live together in Leeds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on the eligibility rules, I'm afraid **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While Child Benefit is automatically available for children under 16, for young people aged 16 or over, they must be under 20 **and** staying in approved education or training for you to continue receiving Child Benefit. Since Alex is 18 but not currently in any approved education or training, the eligibility criteria are not met.

**However**, if Alex's circumstances change and he decides to enroll in approved education or training (such as college, sixth form, or an approved training program), you could become eligible again at that point. If that happens, you would need to contact the Child Benefit Office to update them about his situation.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 but is not currently in approved education or training, the eligibility criteria are not met. Although Alex is not working and not claiming benefits (which would also disqualify eligibility), the requirement for a child over 16 to be in approved education or training is not satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While Alex is under 20 years old, he is not currently in approved education or training, which is a requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 and over. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 but is not currently in approved education or training, the eligibility criteria are not met. Although Alex is not working and not claiming benefits (which would also disqualify eligibility), the requirement for a child over 16 to be in approved education or training is not satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex. While Alex is under 20 years old, he is not currently in approved education or training, which is a requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 and over. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
